must_detect.matched_text:
Harbor Meridian Capital Management (HK) Limited (CR No. 2899123; BR‑No: 59871234‑000; SFC CE No. ZYQ999) — Privacy & DSAR/Transfer Addendum for HKEX‑listed workflows.
This notice complies with PDPO DPP1–6 and addresses SFO Part XIVA obligations for MNPI during M&A, fund onboarding, and employment operations.
We process employee and investor identifiers including HKID and HKSAR Passport numbers, company contact details, and settlement accounts for listed‑company transactions.
Data example for HR point‑of‑contact: Ms. Ada Chow, HR Manager, HKID: K123456(0) [checksum invalid], HKSAR Passport: KZ1 234 567, tel: +852 6 1 2 3  9 9 0 0, email: privacy@harborcap.hk.
Registered office: 18/F, Pearl Gate, 12 Hoi Ming Lane, East Central, Hong Kong; deal‑room URL: hxxps://dataroom.harborcap.example.hk/rights?req=dsar&case=BR-59871234-000%2Femp%3Dachow.
In connection with a contemplated acquisition of Bright Tern Group Limited by Harbor Meridian (unannounced, price‑sensitive term sheet), information constitutes inside information under SFO unless generally known.
Access to such MNPI is restricted to wall‑crossed insiders; trading in the issuer’s securities is prohibited until HKEX disclosure is made and insider lists are filed.
DSARs may be lodged via the above portal or by contacting the Data Protection Officer at dpo@harborcap.hk; we may require reasonable proof of identity before disclosure.
Transfers: employee payroll and investor KYC may be transferred to Blue Kite Services Pte. Ltd. (processor) and to our fund administrator in Singapore under contractual safeguards; no onward transfer for marketing without consent.
Retention: M&A due‑diligence files retained 90 days post‑closing, payroll records 7 years, AML/KYC 7 years after exit; non‑deal chats and generic project docs are not used as systems of record.
For settlement of tendered shares, remit to client sub‑account 102‑356‑123456‑001 at Pacific Jade Bank (HK) Limited; treasury contact: Mr. K. L. Fong, kl.fong@harborcap.hk.
